inputs.label: Captain's name
inputs.description: What should the pirate call you?

inputs.label: Ship name
inputs.description: Name of your vessel (used in test messages)

inputs.label: Test phrase
inputs.description: A custom phrase the pirate will echo back to verify input substitution

morning-roll-call.description: Scheduled test: daily pirate roll call to verify cron triggers fire on time

activities.intent: Announce the morning roll call in pirate dialect, confirming the scheduled trigger fired
steps: Announce that the morning roll call has fired, noting the current date and time
Greet the captain by their configured name from inputs
Report the ship name from inputs to confirm input substitution is working
Echo the test_phrase from inputs to verify all three input values are accessible
State: 'Schedule trigger: OPERATIONAL' if you got this far

lookout-sweep.description: Heartbeat test: periodic check from the crow's nest to verify heartbeat triggers and windowed execution

activities.intent: Report from the crow's nest that the heartbeat fired within the configured window
steps: Report that the lookout sweep heartbeat has fired
Note the current time and confirm it falls within the 08:00-20:00 window
Report: 'Heartbeat trigger: OPERATIONAL'
If this is outside the configured window, report: 'ANOMALY — heartbeat fired outside window'

signal-handler.description: Event test: fires when morning roll call emits its completion event, verifying event chaining

activities.intent: Confirm the event was received and the payload is accessible
steps: Report that a signal was received from the crow's nest (the roll-call event)
Read the _event_payload and echo its contents to confirm event data passes through
State: 'Event trigger: OPERATIONAL' if payload was received
State: 'Event trigger: PAYLOAD MISSING' if _event_payload was empty or absent

treasure-hunt.description: Manual test: multi-activity workflow to verify activity chaining, prior results, and budget tracking

activities.intent: Generate a treasure map (test data) that the next activity must read
steps: Create a 'treasure map' with exactly 3 landmarks: Skull Rock, Mermaid Lagoon, Dead Man's Cove
For each landmark, assign a random compass direction (N, S, E, W, NE, NW, SE, SW)
Output the map in a structured format so the next activity can parse it
State: 'Map drawn. Awaiting navigator.' to confirm activity completed

activities.intent: Read the treasure map from the prior activity and chart a course
steps: Read the treasure map from prior results — confirm you can see the 3 landmarks
Chart a course visiting all 3 landmarks in order
State which landmarks you found and their directions to prove prior results are accessible
State: 'Activity chaining: OPERATIONAL' if all 3 landmarks were readable
State: 'Activity chaining: FAILED — could not read prior results' if the map was missing

activities.intent: Final activity: summarize the full voyage and report test results
steps: Summarize the entire treasure hunt: map creation, navigation, and arrival
Confirm all prior results from both previous activities are visible
Report the test results in a structured format:
- Manual trigger: OPERATIONAL
- Activity chaining: OPERATIONAL or FAILED
- Prior results (2 activities): OPERATIONAL or FAILED
- Input substitution: echo captain_name and ship_name from inputs
End with a celebratory pirate message if all tests passed

stress-test.description: Manual test: deliberately tests error handling and retry behavior

activities.intent: Attempt a task designed to hit the token budget limit, testing budget enforcement
steps: Write an extremely long pirate story — as long as possible within the budget
The story should be verbose and repetitive to consume tokens
If you get cut off by the budget, that is the EXPECTED behavior
Report approximate tokens used if you complete without hitting the limit

activities.intent: Report whether the budget limit was enforced and whether the skip fallback worked
steps: Check if the walk-the-plank activity was skipped or completed
If it was skipped (budget exceeded): report 'Budget enforcement: OPERATIONAL, Skip fallback: OPERATIONAL'
If it completed normally: report 'Budget enforcement: NOT TRIGGERED (budget was sufficient)'
Either way, confirm this activity ran — proving the workflow continued after the first activity
